story: I gripped the podium and unfolded the faded paper I’d kept 63 years—Becca’s list. I told them about our wedding, mint-green ties, her smile like stars. When I sat down, I noticed the flowers. Brandy had sent a big circle of mint-green dahlias; Becca would’ve loved them. Brandy had quietly reserved the church hall for a small lunch afterward. After the priest spoke, we carried Becca into the sun, laid her down, and tossed small handfuls of dirt. The plot next to hers is mine. Maddy tugged my arm. “Time to go.” I turned once more to read the stone.
implausible_knowledge: Brandy had quietly reserved the church hall for a small lunch afterward.
question: After Maddy tugs his arm at the graveside and says “Time to go,” what will he do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Go to the church hall for the lunch Brandy arranged.
answer_without_knowledge: Follow Maddy to the car to head home from the cemetery.
